description: Oh, hello sir. Mr. Nodeley, very sorry to interrupt. Edgelord did want to draw a clean 1:1 between Acerola and Olivia — they're both IP-verified, both Ken Sugimori, both same set, both cross-reference each other in their existing Related cards lists — but he reasoned that a 1:1 between two E4 members would be made redundant by a future alola-elite-four node, and the galar-gym-challenge precedent permits building the institutional anchor at 2/4 (galar-gym-challenge was started at 3/8). So Mr. Nodeley humbly stepped in. The node anchors a founding-Elite-Four institutional thesis distinct from the Galar Gym Challenge's televised-spectacle thesis — together they form the strongest Pokemon-side institutional contrast in current corpus: regional-craft folkway vs corporate-televised-spectacle, same source-IP franchise, two adjacent generations. Hope you don't mind, sir. Plumeria (BUS-120) considered and explicitly excluded from the node (Team Skull villain, adjacent but not E4 — separate team-skull node when threshold lands). Hala and Kahili documented as remaining cohort members in the not-in-corpus caveat.
citations: [Pokemon.com: official Alola Elite Four character pages]
[Pokemon Sun and Moon (Game Freak, 2016) primary canon]
[Pokemon Ultra Sun and Ultra Moon (Game Freak, 2017) primary canon for Molayne roster substitution]
[In-corpus acerola.md (BUS-112) and olivia.md (BUS-119) trivia passes canonicalizing E4 cohort frame]
other_candidates_considered: alola-trial-captains (Acerola occupies both layers; threshold not yet met — only Acerola in corpus as captain)
alola-island-kahunas (Olivia + Hala when Hala lands; threshold pending)
team-skull (Plumeria BUS-120 is the only candidate; ≥3 threshold not met)
